item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I usually downvote whenever I see censored containment procedures but… I feel this is too good of an idea with not enough points so have a +1 from me.
…
But seriously, it's just a bunch of numbers, reveal them and the problem that irks MANY people will be gone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just like the small detail where they checked if they responded to any stimuli. You just know it was some poor assistant who had to do it. "… alright now to for check sexual stimuli. Jeens if you will" "What?! But sir" "No buts Juniour Assisstant Jeens. That comes later. Now get the lube and the magazines…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted because of censorship in the containment procedures. Will change to up if it's fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm betting it's the other half of "brain in a jar" and it's communicating by a sort of modem sing-song.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: been trying to figure this out for a while and unless it's simply the obvious (vat-grown cyborg clone rc spies) i am stumped

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was thinking the same thing as I read it. If I'm new personnel I need to know what I need to watch to maintain secure containment. Other than that I really enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Each cell is to be shielded against radio-frequency transmissions of up to ███ W transmissive power with frequencies ranging from █ Mhz to ██ Ghz. No wireless communication devices are permitted within ███ m of any instance of SCP-1116.

Too many black boxes. Without the figures, these containment procedures are useless for actual containment. Maybe it would be better to replace the black boxes with actual numbers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is some wild fucking shit with all the right "random" notes hit to mesh together and make it more subtly disturbing and I can't believe it's only got +40 votes +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should've done this earlier, but credit should also go to The Raven, as this began as a rewrite of his version of 086. Apologies for not including earlier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After thinking on this and reading Eskobar's excellent Zen And The Art Of DATA REDACTED, I've edited the expungements out the incident report. Lemme know if that works better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The expunged bit is actually less weird than one might expect, but would sound horribly OTT if I actually described it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like how it [DATA EXPUNGED] to kill people (this is seriously overused), but the rest is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We're still working on an actual rewrite. Sorry bout that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reject and I are still working on that rewrite. This one started off as one, but veered off, and Sorts told me I should put it up on its own.

Sorry, didn't mean to step on toes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh…I don't mean to sound like a dick…but wasn't this the in-progress rewrite for 086 that you asked permission for? I don't care that you posted it as a new SCP, but you could at least mention that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, your taste is your taste. One person's existential horror is another's throwaway line. Not offended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry, I don't like to be mean, but I thought this was boring and I'm not sure how it could be less boring. Downvote

edit: I hate to leave it like that… but there just isn't enough here to interest me. There needs to be something deeper for me to get into it. I'm not creeped out, I'm not intrigued, I'm not amused. Those are my three big criteria. One of them has to exist for an upvote for me, but those don't exist here. I need a little more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also why I didn't put in any recovery notes. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Appreciated, guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost want to spoil it by mentioning what the actual idea was, but then again the fact that the Foundation hasn't figured it out was kind of the point. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded. Great job, delta.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was all off for a weekend and didn't get to vote until now, it's good to see that folks liked the idea as much as I did.

No credit my way, this works specifically because you did a good job of writing it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't want to imply they necessarily had any personality to begin with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might be creepier if there were hints that there's still some subjective awareness or vestigial personality in these things, such as pain response or tuneful humming. Maybe if you give one a pencil, the hand draws something while the rest of the body lies stock-still on a table.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh, I'm the same way.

And much better, actually. I'd like to see this in a story, or something more atmospheric.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should also cop to falling for Shown Their Work Syndrome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trimmed that a bit. Better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The CT scan description is what got me interested— the idea that their state was created for unknown use by unknown thing is unsettling. Leaning toward an upvote, but several bits like:

"it has been determined that the cranial antenna of an SCP-1116 specimen can function as a phased array transmitter with beam-forming capabilities. SCP-1116-7 used points of constructive interference while transmitting in the 60 GHz band to produce localized pockets of heated air"

— while certainly clinical in tone, are pretty laborious to read. It's almost too much detail, or too specifically worded, and kills the eerie feeling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow this is really really good. upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks.

I should mention that credit for the idea belongs to Reject & Sorts.

EDIT: And The Raven, as this began as a rewrite of his version of 086. See below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice job, delta_vee, very nice.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice. Spies that can't be pumped for info if captured.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And I upvoted.
